metadata.summary: Patterns for voice UI design: prompt rules, error recovery ladders, multimodal coordination, platform constraints (Alexa, Google, Siri), and key metrics.

content.title: Voice UI Patterns
content.summary: Practical patterns for designing voice user interfaces: prompt writing rules, progressive error recovery (3-step ladder), multimodal voice+screen coordination, platform-specific constraints (Alexa, Google Actions, Siri), and key performance metrics. The core discipline is designing for the ear, not the eye — one idea per turn, concise confirmations only for high-stakes actions, and always providing an escape hatch.

why.p: VUI fails when designed like a GUI: long prompts cause users to forget the start; identical error messages frustrate; missing escape hatches trap users. Empirical VUI research (NNG, Google, Amazon design guides) shows task completion drops sharply after 15 seconds of speech and more than 3-4 options per turn. Progressive error ladders and implicit confirmations for low-risk actions reduce abandonment.

list.item: Designing a new voice skill (Alexa, Google Action, Siri Shortcut, or custom voice bot).
Producing prompt copy, error ladders, and confirmation patterns from a feature spec.
Auditing an existing voice flow against NNG / Google / Amazon design guidelines.
Designing multimodal (voice + screen) flows for display devices.
Pure text chatbots with no audio — use chatbot conversation-design playbooks instead.
IVR with regulatory script requirements (banking, healthcare) — legal-mandated wording overrides UX guidance.
Pre-product validation phase — design utterances on real user data, not imagined scripts.
Languages with limited TTS quality where prosody coaching is futile.

rule.statement: Keep each system turn to one idea; if there are two, ask twice or show the second on screen.
rule.rationale: Users cannot re-read spoken output; two ideas in one turn cause the first to be forgotten by the time the second lands.

rule.statement: Confirm only high-stakes actions (money, deletion); use implicit acknowledgement for low-stakes actions.
rule.rationale: Excessive confirmations train users to stop listening; every extra turn increases abandonment probability.

rule.statement: Keep spoken responses under 15 seconds (~120 words at 150 wpm). If more is needed, summarize and offer detail on request.
rule.rationale: NNG research shows users zone out after 15 seconds of speech; offering detail preserves access without forcing it.

rule.statement: Provide an escape-hatch exit phrase on every node; users must always know how to quit.
rule.rationale: Users trapped in a flow with no exit abandon the product, not just the task.

example.title: Concise implicit ack for low-stakes action
example.code: User: "Add milk" System: "Added milk to your list."

example.title: Verbose, unnecessary confirmation for low-stakes action
example.code: User: "Add milk" System: "I have successfully added milk to your shopping list. Is there anything else you would like to add?"

example.title: High-stakes explicit confirmation
example.code: User: "Send $500 to John" System: "That's $500 to John Smith. Should I send it?"

rule.statement: Vary reprompts across error steps — never use identical wording at step 2 and step 3.
rule.rationale: Repeating the same error message frustrates users who are already stuck; each step must add new information.

example.title: Three-step error ladder for slot capture
example.code: Step 1: "Sorry, I didn't catch that. What city?" Step 2: "I'm still having trouble. You can say a city name like 'New York' or 'Los Angeles'." Step 3: "Let me help differently. Would you like to type the city instead, or speak to someone?"

rule.statement: When voice and screen are both available, speak the top 3 items and show the full list on screen.
rule.rationale: Listing more than 3-4 items verbally exceeds working memory; the screen handles the overflow without overwhelming the ear.

rule.statement: Keep canonical voice+screen pairs in a single JSON source, not parallel documents.
rule.rationale: Parallel docs diverge; a screen that shows different options than the voice prompt confuses users and breaks multimodal trust.

example.title: Voice summarizes, screen shows detail
example.code: Voice: "Here are your upcoming appointments." Screen: [Shows full calendar with details the user can scan] Voice: "Tap the one you want to reschedule." Screen: [Each appointment is tappable]

rule.statement: Preserve conversation context across turns; handle follow-up references without asking users to repeat themselves.
rule.rationale: Forgetting prior turn context is the leading cause of VUI frustration; users expect voice systems to behave like people.

example.title: Implicit update using prior context
example.code: User: "Set an alarm for 7 AM" System: "Alarm set for 7 AM tomorrow." User: "Make it 6:30" System: "Updated to 6:30 AM."

rule.statement: Respect the 8-second response time limit on Alexa; design flows so no single turn requires more processing.
rule.rationale: Alexa kills skills that exceed the time limit; long async operations require progressive disclosure with intermediate acks.

rule.statement: Validate all SSML against the target platform's supported subset before shipping.
rule.rationale: Siri ignores most SSML; Alexa and Google support overlapping but non-identical tag sets; invalid SSML silently degrades to flat TTS.

list.item: Alexa: 8-second response limit, custom wake word not allowed, APL/Card for visual companions, certification required.
Google Actions: multi-surface support (phone/speaker/display), Dialogflow for NLU, rich response cards.
Siri: limited to SiriKit intents + Shortcuts, HomePod integration, privacy-focused with minimal data sharing.

rule.statement: Track task completion rate, average turns per task, error rate, fallback rate, and 7-day retention; act on metrics individually.
rule.rationale: Each metric reveals a different failure mode: high error rate indicates utterance coverage gaps; high turns per task indicates prompt complexity.

list.item: Task completion rate: target above 85%.
Average turns per task: target below 3.
Error rate: target below 15%.
Fallback rate: target below 10%.
User satisfaction (CSAT): target above 4.0/5.
7-day retention: target above 40%.

rule.statement: Track per-node abandonment; if any node exceeds 30%, the prompt fails users — rewrite before adding utterance variants.
rule.rationale: Utterance variants fix recognition; a rewrite fixes comprehension. High abandonment means users understood the system but rejected it.

rule.statement: Provide a visual or text-input alternative for every voice interaction path.
rule.rationale: Speech impediments, accents, quiet environments, and privacy concerns all make voice-only interfaces exclusionary.

rule.statement: Do not auto-deploy voice copy; stage rollouts because one bad utterance lands on all users simultaneously.
rule.rationale: Voice copy ships instantly to all devices — no browser cache to clear, no app update to decline. The blast radius of a bad line is immediate and broad.

antipattern.description: LLM-generated prompts default to verbose, apologetic tone.
antipattern.reason: Explicitly bound max word count and ban filler phrases ("I'd be happy to", "Just to confirm") in the system prompt — the model will not self-edit reliably.

antipattern.description: Generated SSML includes tags rejected by the target platform.
antipattern.reason: Validate every generated SSML fragment against the platform's supported-tag list before shipping; unsupported tags render as silent drops or literal text.

antipattern.description: PII in transcripts sent to LLM for copy "improvement".
antipattern.reason: Redact sensitive slots before any LLM analysis call; build the sanitizer as a mandatory pipeline step, not an optional flag.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use Claude as a "voice pattern librarian" that turns an intent spec into a full conversation graph: greeting, prompt copy, slot capture, confirmation, error ladder (3 steps), help, exit, repeat. A second linter pass scores each utterance on length (≤ 8 s), blame-free framing, example presence, and platform compliance. Output is exported as Voiceflow JSON, Alexa Skills Kit interaction model, or Dialogflow agent. Real-data improvement loop: anonymized analytics feed a "rotate variants" job that swaps fresh utterances when retention drops.

list.item: general-purpose Claude subagent — full conversation graph synthesis.
faion-sdd-executor-agent — implement SDD tasks for the voice agent backend.
A "voice-copy-reviewer" prompt — score / rewrite copy against length + tone rules.
faion-improver skill — sustain weekly improvement loop on retention metrics.
One idea per turn; if there are two, ask twice or show on screen.
Vary reprompts — never identical at step 2 and step 3.
Confirm only high-stakes actions (money, deletion); low-stakes get implicit ack.
Provide an "escape hatch" exit on every node — users must always know how to quit.
Localize prosody, not just words — politeness and pacing vary by language.
Track per-node abandonment; > 30% means the prompt fails users, not vice versa.
ask-cli (Alexa Skills Kit CLI) — Author + simulate Alexa skills
gactions — Google Actions / Conversational Actions
dialogflow (gcloud) — Dialogflow CX agents from CLI
voiceflow API — Export / version conversation graphs
rasa shell / rasa test — Test OSS NLU + policy routing
botium-cli — Cross-platform conversational regression
ElevenLabs / Azure Speech CLI — Generate spoken samples for prosody review
Voiceflow — SaaS; Yes (REST API); Best authoring + agent target
Amazon Alexa Developer Console — SaaS; Yes (ASK CLI); Required for Alexa launch
Google Dialogflow CX — SaaS; Yes (gRPC API); Rich state machine
Apple Siri / SiriKit — OS framework; Limited; Intents extension only
Rasa Open Source — OSS; Yes (REST + CLI); Self-hosted, full control
Botpress — OSS + SaaS; Yes (CLI + REST); LLM-native chatbot platform
Botium — OSS + SaaS; Yes; Conversational regression testing
Dashbot / VoiceLabs — SaaS analytics; Yes (export API); Mine retention + drop-off

template.purpose: Linter that scores voice copy against length (8 s) and blame-free framing rules.
Prompt template for generating a full conversation graph from an intent spec.
